# Hutch

> Hutch is a read-it-later app and browser extension that lets you save articles with one click and read them later. A privacy-first Pocket and Omnivore alternative, built in Australia by the creator of js-cookie.

Hutch provides browser extensions for Firefox and Chrome, a distraction-free reader view, TL;DR summaries, auto dark mode, and secure OAuth authentication with PKCE. User data is hosted in Sydney, Australia under the Australian Privacy Act. No third-party tracking or ads.

Free for the first 100 founding members (lifetime). A$3.99/month after that, including TL;DR summaries.

## Pages

- [Home](https://hutch-app.com/): Landing page with features, pricing, and product overview
- [Install Browser Extension](https://hutch-app.com/install): Download the Hutch extension for Firefox or Chrome
- [Sign Up](https://hutch-app.com/signup): Create a free account and start saving articles
- [Log In](https://hutch-app.com/login): Sign in to your Hutch account
- [Privacy Policy](https://hutch-app.com/privacy): How Hutch handles your data
- [Terms of Service](https://hutch-app.com/terms): Terms governing your use of Hutch

## Optional

- [Privacy Policy](https://hutch-app.com/privacy): Data handling, storage location (AWS Sydney), cookies, and user rights
- [Terms of Service](https://hutch-app.com/terms): Agreement, acceptable use, liability, governing law (Australia)
